title: Hieracium trichodontum
intro: Hieracium trichodontum, also known as rough hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihistaminic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies, wounds, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium trichodontum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Hieracium trichodontum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe, Cichorieae, within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which comprises hawkweeds, a group of plants often known for their similar appearance to dandelions. This plant is a perennial herb that grows in various habitats, including mountainous regions and woodland edges. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium trichodontum is closely related to other dandelion-like plants, such as Taraxacum and Cichorium. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the dandelion tribe.

parts: Hieracium trichodontum, also known as the rough-toothed hawkweed, is a plant species used in traditional medicine.
